title: Warranty Claims Denied Due to Unauthorized Substrate Modifications
failure_mode: Roofing membrane warranty voided after post-installation substrate modifications — HVAC equipment additions, penetration cuts, and overlay patches performed by trades other than the roofing contractor without manufacturer notification or approved repair procedures
frequency: recurring — documented in 9 warranty dispute cases across 5 manufacturers, 2019-2025
notes: Pattern is most common in institutional and commercial buildings where facility maintenance teams cut new penetrations or install rooftop equipment 2-5 years post-occupancy without engaging the original roofing contractor. Manufacturers consistently deny claims citing 'unauthorized alterations' clause. Prevention requires owner education on warranty maintenance requirements and mandatory notification protocols.
